rule_statement: Students and instructors must use task-appropriate personal protective equipment before starting practical work.
when_to_apply: Before any practical task begins and whenever hazards change during the task.
control_actions: Confirm required PPE; brief students on task-specific hazards; stop work if PPE is missing or unsuitable.
evidence_of_compliance: Instructor observation; pre-task check; PPE visible during task.
notes_for_ai: Always include PPE language in Preparation when hazards or tools justify it. Keep wording proportional to the task.
source_basis: ICAO CBTA training emphasis on safe performance; FAA maintenance safety guidance; EASA maintenance safety expectations

rule_statement: The work area, tools, training aids, and documentation must be checked for readiness before task execution.
control_actions: Check housekeeping, lighting, access, tool availability, equipment condition, and correct references.
evidence_of_compliance: Pre-task readiness check; instructor confirmation; completed setup.
notes_for_ai: Usually include as an early Preparation step.
source_basis: FAA AMT safety practices; EASA maintenance data and personnel expectations

rule_statement: Students must use the correct, current training or maintenance reference before and during task execution.
control_actions: Identify the applicable reference; verify revision/status where relevant; prevent work from memory alone.
evidence_of_compliance: Reference visible at workstation; student uses the reference during task; instructor observation.
notes_for_ai: Very important for R/I, FOT, TS, MEL, and LOC tasks.
source_basis: EASA Part-145 maintenance data expectations; FAA documentation and maintenance human factors guidance

rule_name: Isolate electrical power before adjustment or connection changes
rule_statement: Electrical power must be isolated before changing circuit configuration, connecting meters incorrectly could create shock or short-circuit hazards.
when_to_apply: Before configuration changes, meter insertion, component removal/installation, and many troubleshooting steps.
control_actions: Power OFF; verify zero-energy state where applicable; connect measuring devices correctly; re-energize only when required and supervised.
evidence_of_compliance: Observed power isolation; correct meter connection; no live reconfiguration unless planned and controlled.
notes_for_ai: Especially relevant for electrical fundamentals, avionics, and energized troubleshooting activities.
source_basis: FAA electrical safety guidance and AMT maintenance safety practices

rule_name: Never connect an ammeter in parallel
rule_statement: An ammeter must be connected in series only; parallel connection can create a short circuit and damage equipment or injure personnel.
when_to_apply: Electrical measurement exercises and functional test tasks involving current measurement.
control_actions: Instructor briefing before measurements; verify meter mode and leads; isolate circuit before insertion.
evidence_of_compliance: Correct circuit configuration; instructor observation; no equipment damage or unsafe current path.
notes_for_ai: Use when the task involves current measurement.
source_basis: FAA electrical safety guidance; common AMT electrical measurement best practice

rule_name: Control stored energy and movement hazards
rule_statement: Before practical work, identify and control stored energy, unintended movement, or mechanical release hazards.
when_to_apply: Before removal/installation, operational tests, servicing, and troubleshooting on systems with motion or pressure.
control_actions: Depressurize, secure, lock, support, isolate, or otherwise make the system safe before work begins.
evidence_of_compliance: Observed locks, supports, isolation, depressurization, or instructor verification.
notes_for_ai: Include for hydraulics, landing gear, control surfaces, pneumatic, and similar systems.
source_basis: General aviation maintenance safety practice; EASA/FAA maintenance safety expectations

rule_statement: All tools, meters, and loose items must be controlled before, during, and after the task to prevent omission or damage.
control_actions: Count tools where appropriate; return tools to assigned locations; do final check before sign-off.
evidence_of_compliance: Tool check completed; workstation clear; missing items resolved before close-up.
notes_for_ai: Close-up should usually mention tool/equipment check.
source_basis: FAA maintenance human factors and tool control best practice

rule_statement: The work area must be kept clean, organized, and free from foreign object debris or unnecessary clutter.
when_to_apply: Throughout the task and mandatory at close-up.
control_actions: Remove scrap, unused parts, wires, rags, and packaging; keep pathways clear; clean benches and training aids.
evidence_of_compliance: Clean area; no loose debris; instructor close-up inspection.
notes_for_ai: Always suitable for workshop-based training tasks.
source_basis: FAA maintenance safety and human factors guidance

rule_statement: Students must stop and seek instructor guidance when uncertain about a step, reference, or safety condition.
when_to_apply: Any point where uncertainty or abnormal conditions arise.
control_actions: Pause task; consult reference; request instructor check before proceeding.
evidence_of_compliance: Instructor interaction before continuing; observed escalation of uncertainty.
notes_for_ai: Useful as a general note in training-oriented tasks.
source_basis: ICAO CBTA emphasis on safe performance; FAA maintenance human factors guidance

rule_name: Define test conditions before energizing or operating
rule_statement: Functional or troubleshooting activities must define setup, limits, expected indications, and stop criteria before the test begins.
control_actions: Confirm configuration, instrument setup, expected range, abnormal-stop criteria, and instructor supervision.
evidence_of_compliance: Setup brief completed; correct instruments selected; results compared against criteria.
notes_for_ai: Important for FOT and TS tasks to prevent random testing.
source_basis: FAA maintenance test discipline and documentation guidance; EASA maintenance data expectations

rule_name: Control vehicles, carts, and service equipment movement
rule_statement: Ground handling and servicing tasks must control movement, positioning, and connection of support equipment to prevent impact, spills, or unsafe handling.
when_to_apply: Before and during servicing/ground handling activities.
control_actions: Verify positioning, brakes/chocks where applicable, hose/cable routing, communication, and surrounding area clearance.
evidence_of_compliance: Safe equipment setup and monitored handling.
notes_for_ai: Use for SGH tasks and any servicing scenario.
source_basis: General aviation ground servicing best practice; workshop and ramp safety principles

rule_name: Restore system and work area to safe condition
rule_statement: At close-up, the system, training aid, tools, and work area must be restored to a safe, organized, and ready state.
when_to_apply: Part C / Close-up and before sign-off.
control_actions: Power OFF if applicable; disconnect equipment; restore configuration; return items to storage; clean area; final check.
evidence_of_compliance: Observed restoration and instructor confirmation before sign-off.
source_basis: FAA maintenance safety and tool control practice; practical training best practice

rule_name: Adapt safety emphasis to the actual hazards of the task
rule_statement: Safety controls should be proportionate to the actual hazards of the task and environment, not copied blindly from unrelated tasks.
when_to_apply: During task drafting and instructor briefing.
control_actions: Select only relevant warnings, cautions, and notes; avoid overstating hazards that do not apply.
evidence_of_compliance: Task wording matches actual tools, systems, and environment.
notes_for_ai: Prevents generic or unrealistic warnings in generated tasks.
source_basis: SMS risk-based safety thinking; EASA SMS emphasis on accountabilities and risk control

rule_name: Do not continue after unsafe act until corrected
rule_statement: If an unsafe act or unsafe condition is observed during training, the task must be paused and corrected before continuation.
when_to_apply: Any time during task execution or assessment.
control_actions: Stop task; explain issue; correct condition; resume only when safe.
notes_for_ai: Supports formative safety culture and progressive assessment logic.
source_basis: CBTA and safety management principles emphasizing intervention and safe performance
